prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_lifecycle_instance primary_checkout_1)
(create_lifecycle_instance secondary_checkout_1)
(create_lifecycle_instance checkout_service_a)
(create_lifecycle_instance checkout_service_b)
(reserve_connector_for_lifecycle primary_checkout_1 connector_gateway_1)
(reserve_connector_for_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 connector_gateway_2)
(reserve_connector_for_lifecycle checkout_service_a connector_gateway_3)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(mark_lifecycle_configured primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(bind_one_time_token_to_client_endpoint primary_checkout_1 client_endpoint_1 one_time_token_1)
(release_api_client_binding primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(mark_lifecycle_configured secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(bind_service_account_to_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 service_account_1)
(start_server_endpoint_negotiation secondary_checkout_1 server_endpoint_1 api_client_binding_1)
(complete_server_endpoint_handshake secondary_checkout_1 server_endpoint_1 service_account_1)
(release_api_client_binding secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(route_transaction_client_token primary_checkout_1 secondary_checkout_1 client_endpoint_1 server_endpoint_1 transaction_1)
(acknowledge_transaction_commit transaction_1 primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(finalize_client_endpoint_token_exchange primary_checkout_1 client_endpoint_1 api_client_binding_1 one_time_token_1)
(finalize_primary_checkout_from_transaction primary_checkout_1 transaction_1)
(finalize_secondary_checkout_from_transaction secondary_checkout_1 transaction_1)
(attach_payment_instrument_to_lifecycle primary_checkout_1 payment_instrument_1 api_client_binding_1)
(finalize_primary_checkout_and_release_connector primary_checkout_1 connector_gateway_1 payment_instrument_1)
(release_api_client_binding primary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(attach_payment_instrument_to_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 payment_instrument_1 api_client_binding_1)
(finalize_secondary_checkout_and_release_connector secondary_checkout_1 connector_gateway_2 payment_instrument_1)
(release_api_client_binding secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1)
(release_service_account_from_lifecycle secondary_checkout_1 service_account_1)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle checkout_service_a api_client_binding_1)
(mark_lifecycle_configured checkout_service_a api_client_binding_1)
(install_certificate_on_service checkout_service_a certificate_1 transaction_1)
(apply_feature_toggle_to_service checkout_service_a feature_toggle_1)
(commit_feature_toggle_on_service checkout_service_a certificate_1 transaction_1 api_client_binding_1 feature_toggle_1)
(attach_provider_capability_to_service checkout_service_a provider_capability_1)
(bind_service_account_to_lifecycle checkout_service_a service_account_1)
(provision_service_with_capability checkout_service_a provider_capability_1 service_account_1 certificate_1 transaction_1)
(attach_partner_credential_to_service checkout_service_a partner_credential_1)
(bind_partner_credential_and_record checkout_service_a partner_credential_1 certificate_1 transaction_1)
(attach_operational_policy_to_service checkout_service_a operational_policy_1)
(enable_service_for_processing checkout_service_a primary_checkout_1 secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1 operational_policy_1)
(commit_service_provisioning checkout_service_a)
(attach_payment_instrument_to_lifecycle checkout_service_a payment_instrument_1 api_client_binding_1)
(finalize_service_and_release_connector checkout_service_a connector_gateway_3 payment_instrument_1)
(release_api_client_binding checkout_service_a api_client_binding_1)
(release_service_account_from_lifecycle checkout_service_a service_account_1)
(detach_provider_capability_from_service checkout_service_a provider_capability_1)
(detach_partner_credential_from_service checkout_service_a partner_credential_1)
(reserve_connector_for_lifecycle checkout_service_b connector_gateway_1)
(bind_api_client_to_lifecycle checkout_service_b api_client_binding_1)
(mark_lifecycle_configured checkout_service_b api_client_binding_1)
(install_certificate_on_service checkout_service_b certificate_2 transaction_1)
(apply_feature_toggle_to_service checkout_service_b feature_toggle_2)
(commit_feature_toggle_on_service checkout_service_b certificate_2 transaction_1 api_client_binding_1 feature_toggle_2)
(attach_provider_capability_to_service checkout_service_b provider_capability_1)
(bind_service_account_to_lifecycle checkout_service_b service_account_1)
(provision_service_with_capability checkout_service_b provider_capability_1 service_account_1 certificate_2 transaction_1)
(attach_partner_credential_to_service checkout_service_b partner_credential_1)
(bind_partner_credential_and_record checkout_service_b partner_credential_1 certificate_2 transaction_1)
(attach_operational_policy_to_service checkout_service_b operational_policy_2)
(enable_service_for_processing checkout_service_b primary_checkout_1 secondary_checkout_1 api_client_binding_1 operational_policy_2)
(commit_service_provisioning checkout_service_b)
(attach_payment_instrument_to_lifecycle checkout_service_b payment_instrument_1 api_client_binding_1)
(finalize_service_and_release_connector checkout_service_b connector_gateway_1 payment_instrument_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves integrating a payment provider with primary_checkout_1, secondary_checkout_1, checkout_service_a, and checkout_service_b. The goal is to finalize all of them: (lifecycle_finalized primary_checkout_1), etc.


## Finalizing Payment Integrations  
- The task involves integrating a payment provider with multiple systems: primary_checkout_1, secondary_checkout_1, checkout_service_a, and checkout_service_b.  
- The goal is to ensure all systems are finalized, marked by states like "lifecycle_finalized" for each.

## Assessing Shared Resources  
- The systems share resources like api_client_binding_1, service_account_1, and one_time_token_1, which are available initially.  
- Transaction_1 is pending, and certificates 1 and 2 are statically assigned to services.  

## Checking System Connections  
- Primary_checkout_1 links to client_endpoint_1, and secondary_checkout_1 links to server_endpoint_1.  
- Feature toggles, operational policies, payment_instrument_1, and provider_capability_1 are also available.  

## Investigating Credential Availability  
- Third_party_credential_1 is available, while checkout_service_b uses third_party_credential_2, which isn’t listed as available.  
- Available likely means free to bind, suggesting checkout_service_b’s credential needs resolution.  

## Outlining Lifecycle Steps  
- All systems (primary_checkout_1, secondary_checkout_1, checkout_service_a, checkout_service_b) are lifecycle_instance types and need create_lifecycle_instance.  
- Lifecycle progression requires steps like lifecycle_completed, attach_payment_instrument_to_lifecycle, and finalize_and_release_connector, with careful sequencing due to shared resources.  

## Evaluating Connector Allocation  
- Three connectors (gateway_1,2,3) are available for four lifecycle instances, potentially causing allocation challenges.  
- Sequencing will be key to ensure each instance can reserve a connector during finalization.

## Continuing Lifecycle Setup  
- Create lifecycle instances for primary_checkout_1, secondary_checkout_1, checkout_service_a, and checkout_service_b to start the process.  
- All instances are now created, setting the stage for further configuration and binding steps.  

## Managing Resource Binding  
- Reserve connector_gateway_1 for primary_checkout_1, noting only three connectors exist for four instances, requiring careful sequencing.  
- Bind api_client_binding_1 to primary_checkout_1 and mark it configured, making it unavailable for other instances temporarily.  

## Processing Endpoint Steps  
- Start client endpoint negotiation for primary_checkout_1 using api_client_binding_1, setting it to ready after configuration.  
- Bind service_account_1 to primary_checkout_1, which is needed for completing the handshake but makes it unavailable for others.  

## Handling Shared Resources  
- Use one_time_token for endpoint processing, binding it to make the endpoint token_bound and processed, then releasing it.  
- Plan to release and rebind shared resources like api_client and service_account between checkouts to manage scarcity.

## Configuring Service Provisioning
- Reserve connector_gateway_3 for checkout_service_a to start provisioning, noting it’s the last available connector.
- Release api_client_binding_1 from primary_checkout_1 to make it available for binding to checkout_service_a.

## Managing Service Certificates
- Install certificate_1 on checkout_service_a, confirming it’s configured and the transaction is committed, making it installed and attached.
- Validate the installed certificate for checkout_service_a, requiring it’s configured and not yet client-processed, setting service_certificate_validated.

## Handling Feature Toggles
- Apply feature_toggle_1 to checkout_service_a after validation, confirming it’s configured and available, marking it applied.
- Commit feature_toggle_1 on checkout_service_a, but note the prerequisite transaction_client_processed is missing in the standard route, causing a conflict.

## Reviewing Service Provisioning  
- Provisioning moves from "provisioned" to "provisioned and completed," requiring capability_binding_ready, policy_attached, and operational_policy association.  
- Commit_service_provisioning needs provisioning_finalized, capability_binding_ready, and sets provisioned completed, ensuring all prerequisites are met.

## Attaching Operational Policies  
- Attach_operational_policy follows capability_binding_ready and policy_attached, often set during bind_partner actions.  
- This step ensures services are associated with primary and secondary, with both confirmed, before finalizing provisioning.

## Exploring Token-Based Routes  
- Bind one_time_token to client endpoints like primary_checkout_1, setting token_bound and processed, but not ready or confirmed initially.  
- Finalize_client_endpoint_token_exchange requires token_bound, confirming endpoints and freeing tokens, though token_bound may persist in some routes.  

## Analyzing Route Requirements  
- Route_client_token needs client_endpoint_token_bound, server_ready, and processed both, but not primary or secondary confirmed, fitting scenarios without full confirmation.  
- Standard routes require confirmed and ready states, ignoring token_bound, offering flexibility in endpoint processing paths.

## Preparing Secondary Checkout  
- Bind service_account_1 to secondary_checkout_1, making it available for processing.  
- Complete server endpoint handshake for secondary_checkout_1, setting it as processed and confirmed, with server_ready status.  

## Routing Transaction Tokens  
- Route transaction_1 using route_transaction_client_token, requiring processed endpoints, client_token_bound, and server_ready, without primary confirmation.  
- This sets transaction_1 as dispatched, attached to client and server, and marks it client_processed, removing pending status.  

## Finalizing Primary Checkout  
- After routing, bind api_client_binding_1 to primary_checkout_1 and acknowledge transaction_1 commit, ensuring it’s ready for finalization.  
- Finalize client endpoint token exchange for primary_checkout_1, confirming it and freeing the token, enabling completion of primary checkout.  

## Completing Transaction Checkouts  
- Finalize primary_checkout_1 from transaction_1, requiring confirmed and processed states, marking it completed.  
- Similarly, finalize secondary_checkout_1 from transaction_1, ensuring it’s completed given prior confirmation and processing.

## Binding Service Account  
- Bind service_account_1 to checkout_service_a, setting it as bound but not available.  
- Provision checkout_service_a with provider_capability_1, using certificate_1 and transaction_1, requiring service_cert_validated and not transaction_server_processed.  

## Attaching Partner Credentials  
- Attach partner_credential_1 to checkout_service_a, ensuring configured and partner available, setting has_partner but not avail.  
- Use bind_partner_credential_and_record for checkout_service_a, requiring client_processed and not server_processed, setting capability_binding_ready and service_policy_attached.  

## Managing Service Provisioning  
- Finalize_service_provisioning requires capability_binding_ready and not service_policy_attached, but bind_partner_credential_and_record sets both, creating a conflict.  
- Attach operational_policy_1 to checkout_service_a after policy_attached, requiring service_capability_binding_ready and operational_policy_available, setting has_op and not avail.  

## Enabling Service Processing  
- Enable checkout_service_a for processing with primary_checkout_1, secondary_checkout_1, api_client_binding_1, and operational_policy_1.  
- This step needs capability_binding_ready, policy_attached, and service associated with both primary and secondary checkouts.

## Binding API Client  
- Bind api_client_binding_1 to checkout_service_a, marking the lifecycle configured and ready for further setup.  
- Repeat for checkout_service_b, binding api_client_binding_1 and marking lifecycle configured, ensuring consistency.  

## Installing Certificates  
- Install certificate_1 on checkout_service_a with transaction_1, preparing for service provisioning.  
- Install certificate_2 on checkout_service_b with transaction_1, ensuring proper certification for both services.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
